name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 24.11.2025

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 24.11.2025.pdf

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Rabi Sowing Status: As on November 21, 2025, the area sown under Rabi crops is 306.31 lakh ha (including 73.80 lakh ha in Rapeseed & Mustard & 53.71 lakh ha in Gram) as compared to 272.78 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Rabi Sowing Status: As on November 21, 2025, the area sown under Rabi crops is 306.31 lakh ha (including 73.80 lakh ha in Rapeseed & Mustard & 53.71 lakh ha in Gram) as compared to 272.78 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As  on  21.11.2025,  71.68%  of  area  has  been  harvested  for  Kharif  crops  all  over  India. Approximately, 60% area of Pulses mainly in Uradbean (93%), Moongbean (99%) & Mothbean (100%);  92.54%  area  of  Oilseeds  majorly  in  Groundnut  (92.57%),    Soyabean  (99.98%)& Sesamum (89.86%); 82.91% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals, majorly in Jowar (86.10%) & Bajra (95.38%) and 52% area of Rice has been harvested for crops of Kharif 2025-26.
texts.text:  As  on  21.11.2025,  71.68%  of  area  has  been  harvested  for  Kharif  crops  all  over  India. Approximately, 60% area of Pulses mainly in Uradbean (93%), Moongbean (99%) & Mothbean (100%);  92.54%  area  of  Oilseeds  majorly  in  Groundnut  (92.57%),    Soyabean  (99.98%)& Sesamum (89.86%); 82.91% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals, majorly in Jowar (86.10%) & Bajra (95.38%) and 52% area of Rice has been harvested for crops of Kharif 2025-26.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update :
texts.text: 2. Weather Update :

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Minimum temperature is likely to be below normal in many parts of Central India, West India, some parts of NW India, GWB, Jharkhand, Kerala and Rayalaseema. TMAX is likely  to  be  above  normal  in  J&K,  Himachal  Pradesh,  West  Rajasthan,  Bihar, some parts of East U.P., Arunachal Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, Tamilnadu and South Karnataka .
texts.text: Temperature: Minimum temperature is likely to be below normal in many parts of Central India, West India, some parts of NW India, GWB, Jharkhand, Kerala and Rayalaseema. TMAX is likely  to  be  above  normal  in  J&K,  Himachal  Pradesh,  West  Rajasthan,  Bihar, some parts of East U.P., Arunachal Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, Tamilnadu and South Karnataka .

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall  Distribution: From  October  01-  November  23  2025,  the  All-India  cumulative rainfall  is  26%  higher  than  its  Long  Period  Average (LPA), with regional variations:  (i) East & Northeast India: 10%; (ii) Northwest India: 94%; (iii) Central India: 48%; and South Peninsula: 8%.
texts.text: Rainfall  Distribution: From  October  01-  November  23  2025,  the  All-India  cumulative rainfall  is  26%  higher  than  its  Long  Period  Average (LPA), with regional variations:  (i) East & Northeast India: 10%; (ii) Northwest India: 94%; (iii) Central India: 48%; and South Peninsula: 8%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The Well-Marked low-pressure area over Strait of Malacca and adjoining South Andaman Sea on 23 rd  November. It is very likely to intensify into a depression over southeast Bay of Bengal  and  adjoining  south  Andaman  Sea  on  24 th   November.  It  is  likely  to  further intensify into a cyclonic storm over southeast Bay of Bengal during subsequent 48 hours.
texts.text:  The Well-Marked low-pressure area over Strait of Malacca and adjoining South Andaman Sea on 23 rd  November. It is very likely to intensify into a depression over southeast Bay of Bengal  and  adjoining  south  Andaman  Sea  on  24 th   November.  It  is  likely  to  further intensify into a cyclonic storm over southeast Bay of Bengal during subsequent 48 hours.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  A Low pressure area is likely to form over Comorin and adjoining areas of southwest Bay of Bengal and Sri Lanka around 25 th  November
texts.text:  A Low pressure area is likely to form over Comorin and adjoining areas of southwest Bay of Bengal and Sri Lanka around 25 th  November

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Fairly  widespread  to  widespread  rainfall  activity  likely  over  Tamil  Nadu,  Kerala  and Andaman &Nicobar Islands.
texts.text:  Fairly  widespread  to  widespread  rainfall  activity  likely  over  Tamil  Nadu,  Kerala  and Andaman &Nicobar Islands.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated to Scattered rainfall activity over Coastal Andhra Pradesh, Coastal Karnataka and South Interior Karnataka.
texts.text:  Isolated to Scattered rainfall activity over Coastal Andhra Pradesh, Coastal Karnataka and South Interior Karnataka.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Gradual  fall  in  minimum  temperature  likely  by  2-3 0 C  over  many  plains  of  northwest India and Maharashtra during next 3 days and no large change thereafter.
texts.text:  Gradual  fall  in  minimum  temperature  likely  by  2-3 0 C  over  many  plains  of  northwest India and Maharashtra during next 3 days and no large change thereafter.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  No large change in the minimum temperature likely over rest part of the country
texts.text:  No large change in the minimum temperature likely over rest part of the country

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  20  November  2025,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  161 important  reservoirs  across  the  country  (monitored  by  CWC)  is  161.820  billion  cubic meter,  which  is  88.7%  of  total  live  storage  capacity  (182.479  BCM).  The  current  year's storage is  nearly 106.52 percent of last  year's storage  and 120.72 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  20  November  2025,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  161 important  reservoirs  across  the  country  (monitored  by  CWC)  is  161.820  billion  cubic meter,  which  is  88.7%  of  total  live  storage  capacity  (182.479  BCM).  The  current  year's storage is  nearly 106.52 percent of last  year's storage  and 120.72 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater  Levels :  In  August  2025,  around  89%  of  the  country's  monitoring  stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
texts.text: Groundwater  Levels :  In  August  2025,  around  89%  of  the  country's  monitoring  stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh during August 2025 has been significantly influenced by the over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.
texts.text:  The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh during August 2025 has been significantly influenced by the over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Annual water level comparison with the previous year August 2024 to August 2025 has shown  that  about  56.5%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country  experienced  rise  in annual water level fluctuation because of the higher rainfall in 2025 compared to 2024 in Monsoon period.
texts.text:  Annual water level comparison with the previous year August 2024 to August 2025 has shown  that  about  56.5%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country  experienced  rise  in annual water level fluctuation because of the higher rainfall in 2025 compared to 2024 in Monsoon period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  August 2025.
texts.text:  About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  August 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against  a  requirement  of  164.05  lakh  quintals, ensuring  a  surplus of  14.60  lakh  quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against  a  requirement  of  164.05  lakh  quintals, ensuring  a  surplus of  14.60  lakh  quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests & Diseases : Intensity of pests and diseases at some fields has been reported to be above Economic Threshold Level for Chilli (Black thrips) in Uttar Pradesh. The Advisory has been issued  by  the  survey  team  to  the  farms  and  regular  monitoring  is  being  carried  out.  The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests & Diseases : Intensity of pests and diseases at some fields has been reported to be above Economic Threshold Level for Chilli (Black thrips) in Uttar Pradesh. The Advisory has been issued  by  the  survey  team  to  the  farms  and  regular  monitoring  is  being  carried  out.  The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  During the week (13 Nov - 19 Nov 2025), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 71%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st Oct to 19 Nov 2025) is higher than LPA by 27%.
texts.text:  During the week (13 Nov - 19 Nov 2025), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 71%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st Oct to 19 Nov 2025) is higher than LPA by 27%.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rainfall Forecast:
texts.text: Rainfall Forecast:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Overall, below normal rainfall is likely over most parts of India during the week, except in many parts of  Northeast  and  East  India,  and  some  parts  of  Andhra  Pradesh,  where  the rainfall is likely to be normal to above normal.
texts.text: Overall, below normal rainfall is likely over most parts of India during the week, except in many parts of  Northeast  and  East  India,  and  some  parts  of  Andhra  Pradesh,  where  the rainfall is likely to be normal to above normal.
